suggestion_reasons.Agency-Centered Framing: This criterion is working exactly as intended — the rubric-guided draft scored highest and the gap from the generic draft was the widest of any criterion, confirming it's the most differentiating criterion in the rubric. Kept entirely unchanged.
suggestion_reasons.Scene-Level Specificity: The rubric-guided and original preference drafts tied at the top, meaning this criterion is capturing the user's intent well even if it isn't the primary differentiator. Kept entirely unchanged.
suggestion_reasons.Donor as Strategic Co-Architect: This criterion produced the largest differentiation gap (5 vs. 1 vs. 2) and is the clearest reason the rubric-guided draft ranked first — but the original preference draft scored only a 2, meaning it almost entirely missed this criterion before the rubric named it. The user's comments confirm this is the most important framing distinction ('The donor is framed as a strategic investor, not a charitable giver'), so priority is raised from 4 to 2 to reflect that it is functionally co-equal with agency framing in shaping the overall quality of a piece.
suggestion_reasons.Emotionally Honest Tone: The rubric-guided draft scored highest and the user specifically called out 'early data' honesty and urgency precision as strengths, confirming this criterion is working. Kept entirely unchanged.

suggestion_reasons.Agency-Centered Framing: Already scoring at its highest on the rubric-guided draft and the user explicitly called out agency framing as central to why that draft won — kept exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Donor as Strategic Co-Architect: Already scoring at its highest on the rubric-guided draft and the user named strategic investor framing versus charitable framing as a core reason for their ranking — kept exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Narrative Through-Line: Already scoring at its highest on the rubric-guided draft and the user praised the structural echo between subject line and closing line as a specific craft achievement — kept exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Scene-Level Specificity: The original preference draft outscored the rubric-guided draft here (4 vs. 3), and the user explicitly praised the donuts-and-Wednesday-mornings detail as 'exactly the kind of specific human texture I love' — yet the rubric-guided draft still won overall. The current description already names the right target but the dimensions don't flag the distinction between conceptually vivid framing and physically observable, sensory detail. Added a new dimension to make that distinction explicit so future rubric-guided drafts don't trade concrete physical grounding for abstract conceptual clarity.
suggestion_reasons.Emotionally Honest Tone: Already scoring at its highest on the rubric-guided draft and the user called out the closing urgency framing — capability waiting, not suffering for inaction — as a specific strength, confirming this criterion is well-calibrated — kept exactly as-is.
